inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. B. 82 of 1953-54.)
Niḍigallu, Venkatagiri Division, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone near the steps of the sluice of the tank.
Dated in Śaka 1448, Vyaya, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, Monday, corresponding to 1526 A.D., July 23.

Damaged. Seems to record the construction of a sluice (Kaluṁju) to the tank at Niḍaṁgaṇṭi
-Māvuram by Iṅgumānanēniṁgāru (?), the Kāryakarttā of Tirumala-nāyiniṅgāru, son of Yeggappa-
nāyiniṅgāru and the officer-in-charge of Chandragiri-durgam for the merit of the latter and king
Kṛishṇarāya. It is stated that Niḍaṁgallu lies in Kalimili(?)-sīma.

edition.p: Completely Damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. B. 85 of 1953-54.)
Niḍigallu, Venkatagiri Division, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the Dhavajastaṁbha in front of the Kēśava temple.
inscription.h4: Mādirāja, son of Rāmayadēva Chōḍa mahārāja.
inscription.p:
Dated in Śaka 1353 (wrong for 1351), Saumya, Vaiśākha, śu. [5] Thursday, corresponding to
1429 A.D., April 8 (Friday). In the third face, the record refers to a chronogram: dvaya-śara-trīndu
denoting Śaka 1352.

Damaged. Records the erection of a pillar by the chief in front of the temple of
Aśvatthanārāyaṇa of Niḍiṁgallu.

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. B. 87 of 1953-54.)
Dimilāḍa, Tekkali Taluk, Srikakulam District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar, left of the entrance to the Trimūrti temple.
Undated. In characters of 11th century A.D.

Records the gift of a perpetual lamp to god Vishṇu of Dimili-vāḍa by a person, Kēta
Mārapōta and his wife.
(Published in JAHRS, Vol. IV, pp. 163-164).

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. B. 88 of 1953-54.)
Dimilāḍa, Tekkali Taluk, Srikakulam District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar, left of the entrance to the Trimūrti temple (below No. 56: A.R. 87 of 1953-1954.)
Dated in Śaka 1053, Mahādvādaśī, corresponding to 1131 A.D.

Records the gift of a perpetual lamp (to god Vishṇu) by a son (name not clear) of Sōmaya
of Śāṇḍilya-gōtra, and his wife.
(Published in JAHRS, Vol. IV, pp. 163-164).

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. B. 91 of 1953-54.)
Tekkali, Tekkali Taluk, Srikakulam District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone in the palace of the Raja Sahib.
Dated in Śaka 10[53], corresponding to 1131 A.D.

Damaged. Refers to the construction of a temple at • • • • in Sāgara-Khonna-vishaya and
the consecration of a deity therein by the queen • • • • jella-mahādēvī and her gift of a perpetual
lamp to the same.

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. B. 4 of 1954-55.)
Mōṭupalli, Bapatla Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the north wall (base-tiers) of the Rāmasvāmin temple.

Durmati (Dundubhi), Āshāḍha, ba. 2, Thursday, probably equivalent to A.D. 1322, July 1. (in
characters of 14th century A.D.)

Records the consecration of the god Gōpīnātha by Dharma Rāmaseṭṭi. Another inscription
refers to one Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara (name lost).

edition.p: On the northern base of the Rāmasvāmi shrine.
edition.l:
du[ṁ]¨rmmati saṁvatsara ·āṣāḍha ba 2 guruvārāna dha¨rmmarāmaseṭṭi tirupratiṣṭa(ṣṭha)
jēsina śrī gō[pī]nāthuniki golla [ṭhis ·inscription ·ends ·abruptly.]
edition.p: On the base of Rāmasvāmi temple.

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. B. 5 of 1954-55.)
Mōṭupalli, Bapatla Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab on the pavement in the Vīrabhadra temple.
Śaka 1[117] Vishu sankrānti. (A.D. 1195).
Damaged and fragmentary. Details lost.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. B. 7 of 1954-55.)
Nāgārjunikoṇḍa (Nāgulavaram), Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: Inscribed fragment recovered from the excavation site No. 7.

Undated. Palaeographically datable to the Brāhmī of 3rd century A.D. Written in the Prākṛit
language.

Refers to the payment of a dīnāra-māshaka for maintaining an endowment for the feeding
and clothing, apparently of monks. Mentions Kalūra [and Buddhist sect apara-mahā-sēliya?].

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. B. 8 of 1954-55.)
Nāgārjunikoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: Inscribed fragment (No. 1) from a field near the palace area of excavation site No. 7.

Undated. Palaeographically attributable to the Brāhmī of 3rd century A.D. Written in the
Prākṛit language.
Seems to refer to the gift of a pillar.

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. B. 9 of 1954-55.)
Nāgārjunikoṇḍa Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: Inscribed fragment (No. 2) from a field near the palace area of excavation site No. 7.

Undated. Palaeographically datable to the Brāhmī of 3rd century A.D. Written in the Prākṛit
language.
Seems to refer to the gift of a pillar. Mentions a Kulaputra.

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. B. 10 of 1954-55.)
Nāgārjunikoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: Inscribed fragment (No. 3) from a field near the palace area of excavation site No. 7.

Dated in regnal year . ., grīshma, paksha 2, divasa 1. Palaeographically datable to the Brāhmī
of 3rd century A.D. Written in the Prākṛit language.

Mentions the vīhāra to the east of Vijayapurī on the Chuladhammagiri (Published in Ep.
Ind., XXXIV, pp. 207 ff).

l.pr: gimha pakha bitiyaṁ divasa pathamaṁ Si

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. B. 12 of 1954-55.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured slab in the Museum.

Undated. Palaeographically datable to the Brāhmī script of 1st-2nd century A.D. Written in
the Prākṛit language.

Records the gift of a pillar. Mentions thera Ayira Bhūtarakhita and Ayira Budharakhita.
(J. Burgess, ASI, Vol. I, p. 104, No. 39).

l.pr: diyaṁ tiga sa yā yaṁ • • thabhō dā[naṁ]

inscription.head:
No. 65A.
(A.R. No. B. 13 of 1954-55.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured slab in the Museum.

Undated. Palaeographically datable to the Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D. Written in the
Prākṛit language.

Mentions some personal names like Bāndhika (son of Nakilavāni) and Vidhira, Budharakhita,
Saṁgitā and also the Chula-saṁgha (small monastery) and a gahapati(?).

l.pr:
Sidhaṁ namō bhagavatā Jaṁbud[ī]pa-dāsaka bhaga[va] dhātu-parikamena
mahāchētīyā Nakilavāni-puta sa-Bāṁdhikasa saṁghakasa sa-mātuka[sa]

bhāginēyasa Budharakhita matiyaratasa Vidhirasa Saṁgitākasa-sa-mātukasa sa-bhāyakasa
sa-putānaṁchātaṁ saṁghasa chula-sagha[saṁ]

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. B. 14 of 1954-55.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured slab in the Museum.

Undated. Datable to late 2nd century Brāhmī script. Written in an admixture of Prākṛit and
Sanskrit.

Seems to record some gift by Gōtamīputra Yajña Śātakarṇi (the 27th ruler of the Purāṇic list
of Andhra-Śātavāhana kings). Refers to Dhanakaṭa-Chaitya.

l.pr:
Sidhaṁ rājñaḥ Gōtami[putrasya ?] • Ya[jña] Sāta[ka] • • • • • • • • • • [puradēvasa]
• • • • • jjaya • • • thasa • •
jayilananaṁ nadipute • ma dhālayaḥ raṁña • • • • • • • • • • sva • si • • • • • vihārayi

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. B. 15 of 1954-55.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured slab in the Museum.
Palaeographically datable to the Telugu-Kannaḍa characters of 12th-13th century A.D.
Badly damaged. Seems to refer to some gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. B. 16 of 1954-55.)
Dharaṇikōṭa, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab in the house of Kakkapilla Babayya.

Undated. Belongs to 11th-12th century Telugu-Kannada characters. Written in Sanskrit and
Telugu.

Fragmentary. Gives the genealogy of the chief Rājēndra Chōḍa of the Velanāṭi family. Refers
to the originator of the family as Mallavarman, who obtained Velanāṇḍu-vishaya from Tryaṁbaka
Pallava for the assistance rendered in the latter's wars.

edition.p: Figures of crescent-moon, liṅga, sun in one row and that of Nandin below to the left.

l.te: rādhitāt prāptōyaṁ velanāṇḍu nāma viṣaya ssā-
vaṁśajātēṣu tatō nṛpēṣu gatēṣvanēkēṣu vipakṣa bhūpama-
sita yaśō pōta dikcakravālō goṁkaḥ śrīmā-
tasya nṛpuṁgavasya goṁkakṣitīśasya śaśāṁka vaktrā sa-

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. B. 17 of 1954-55.)
Dharaṇikōṭa, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone near Bathina Musalayya's house.
Dated Śaka 1141, Māgha, ba. 2, Thursday (1223 A.D., January 19).

Registers the gift of 55 goats for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Amarēśvara of Dannāḍa
by Māra-boya, son of Chandana-boya.

edition.p: Figures of sun, liṅga and crescent-moon and bull (below sun.)

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. B. 18 of 1954-55.)
Dharaṇikōṭa, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a fragment of a slab in a private house (impressions from Mr. P.S. Sastri, Guntur).
Palaeographically datable to Brāhmī of 3rd century A.D. Written in the Prākṛit language.
Seems to record the gift of a pillar by the wife of a person (name lost).

edition.p:
a) i) nomaya Kuḷaka
ii) bhariyāya Bala ma - in 3rd century A.D.
iii) thabho

b) ma [Figure of Svastika] (Letter ma of 2nd-1st centuries B.C.). Design below the
above letter ma and svastika.
c) [Śrī Varakaṁ] (in 4th-5th century Telugu-Kannaḍa characters)
d) & e) Shell characters

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. B. 21 of 1954-55.)
Jonnavāḍa, Kovur Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab in the Kāmākshi-amman Sannidhi street.
Dated in Śaka 1709 (read in A.R. as 1706), Krōdhi, Chaitra śu. 15, Monday. Late inscription.

States that Venkaṭēśvara Pauṇḍrika, son of Abbājī-Paṇḍita (?) and grandson of Jagganna-
dīkshita of Āpastaṁba-sūtra and Yajus-śākhā performed the Mahāgnichayana sacrifice.

edition.p: Figures of sun and crescent-moon.

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. B. 22 of 1954-55.)
Komarapūḍi, Nellore Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the beam at the entrance of the Narasiṁhasvāmin temple on the Narasiṁhakoṇḍa hill.
Dated in Piṅgaḷa, Kārtika, śu. 15 (in the 16th-17th century characters).

Records the gift of a tank to the god by the chief. (cf. Inscriptions of the Nellore District,
Part II, pp. 804-805, No. 35). He is described as a mahāmaṇḍalēśvara and bears the epithets, Chāḷukya-
nārāyaṇa, Chauhattamalla and raṇavijaya.

l.te:
piṁggaḷa saṁvatsara kā¨rttika śu 15 lu śrīmanmahāmaṁḍḍalēśvara cā[ḷi]kya nārāyaṇa
cauhattamalla-
raṇavijaya velamapāṭi tirumalarājayadēva mahārājuṁgāru vēdādri śrīnārasiṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. B. 24 of 1954-55.)
Lēbūru, Nellore Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p:
On a wooden plank in the garbhagṛiha of the Kunkāḷamma temple.
. • • • .
Dated in Śaka 1695, Durmukhi, Jyēshṭha, śu. 15 (late characters).

Records the gift of a garden by Bachchana to the deity Kunkāḷamma(?). [A.R. states that the
image of the goddess was painted with lacquer on the specified date.]

l.te:
·agunēṭi || du¨rmmukhi nāma saṁvvatsa[ra jyē]ṣṭha śu 15 śrī kuṁkkāḷamma baccana
cēśi(si)-

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. B. 28 of 1954-55.)
Lēbūru, Nellore Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p:
On a pillar left of the entrance to the Vālīśvara temple.
. • • .
Undated. Palaeographically datable to 16th-17th century A.D.

Seems to refer to a levy of grain on the lands irrigated by the tank of Lēbūru. (Inscriptions
of the Nellore District, Part II, p. 810, No. 43).

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. B. 29 of 1954-55.)
Lēbūru, Nellore Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p:
On a pillar, left of the entrance to the Vālīśvara temple.
. • • .
Undated and fragmentary. Written in late characters.

Contains only the imprecatory portion. (Inscriptions of the Nellore District, Part II, p. 811, No.
44).

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. B. 30 of 1954-55.)
Lēbūru, Nellore Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p:
On the lintel of the gōpura of the Chennakēśavasvāmin temple.
. • • • .
Dated in Śaka 1733, Prajōtpatti.

States that Nārāyaṇa-reḍḍi, son of Yaram-reḍḍi-Sūrāreḍḍi built the temple. The work was
executed by Chennakēśava, son of Rudrābattu of Kannamūru.

edition.p: Figures of sun and crescent-moon.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. B. 31 of 1954-55.)
Lēbūru, Nellore Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured slab fixed into the gōpura of the Chennakēśavasvāmin temple.

Dated in Śaka 15[77], Manmatha, Jyēshṭha, śu. 15 corresponding to A.D. 1655, June
8.

Seems to refer to Kandamūri Vīrapa, probably as the sculptor who made the image of
Chennakēśava.

edition.p:
Figures of Garuḍa, Āñjanēya and Chennakēśava (flanked by Chauri-bearers on either
side).

l.te:
śrī rāma || svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu 15[77] ·agunēṭi
manmatha saṁvvatsara jēṣṭa [śu] 15 lu kaṁdamūri vīrapa • • • • • • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. B. 34 of 1954-55.)
Nellore, Nellore Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: To the right of the entrance into the main shrine in the Vēṇugōpālasvāmin temple.

Dates: (i) Śaka 1828, Viśvāvasu, Kārttika, śu. 13, Thursday, A.D. 1906. (Late
inscription).
(ii) Śaka 1847, Raktākshi, Vaiśākha, śu. 1, Sunday, A.D. 1925. (Late inscription).

States that Śrī-Nārāyaṇa-Rāmanuja-Jīyar of the Yadugiri-Yatirāja-maṭha of Tirunārāyaṇapuram-
Mēlukōṭa in Mahisūrarājya in Karnāṭa-maṇḍala, established by Srī Rāmānujachārya, on his return from
pilgrimage to Badarikāśrama in the north, having camped at Siṁhapuram alias Nellūru, worshipped
god Vēṇugōpālasvāmin consecrated in Mūlasthānēśvarapēṭa (Mūlapēṭa) and established certain practices
according to the Pāñcharātr-āgama in the temple and that Saṁpatkumāra-Rāmānuja-jīyar, the fourth
pontiff after the former, visited the place, while on a pilgrimage to the north in the Śaka year 1847,
Raktākshi, Vaiśākha, śu. 1, Sunday, received the honours due to him from the maṭha established
there by his predecessor and ordered the recording of this inscription on stone. The signatures of the
Dharmakarttas are appended below the inscription.

edition.p: Figures of discus, tīrunāmam and conch.

l.note: A little space is left out before this word.
Brackets are found in the inscription, as this is an inscription of 20th
Commas are found in the inscription, as this is an inscription of 20th

inscription.p:
Right side of the foot-steps near the tomb in Daragāmiṭṭa.

(Inscriptions of the Nellore District, Part II, p. 817, No. 49).

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. B. 42 of 1954-55.)
Pallepāḍu, Nellore Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone lying in the compound of the Rāmēśvarasvāmin temple.

Dated in Śaka 14[27] for 1426 Raktākshin, Vaiśākha, śu. 7, Wednesday, corresponding to A.D.,
1504, April 21. The week-day however was Sunday.

States that Boppuḍi Vīramarasa, son of Venkaṭaya and Vīramāṁbā, grandson of Sūraparāja
and Ōbhuḷamma and great grandson of Satyarāja Ōbhaḷayya of Bhāradvāja-gōtra, Yajus-śākhā and
Āpastaṁba-sūtra, built the temple of Rāmēśvara at Pallipāḍu.

l.note:
Above this line, the following line is incised, probably at a later period:[muccikrōla]
ggalyagāri kaṇa .

l.te: vaiśākha śu 7 bu nāṁḍu puṣyā nakṣatrāna dakṣa(kṣi)ṇa kailā-

Urajām, Srikakulam Taluk, Srikakulam District, Andhra Pradesh.

p.i: Eastern Gaṅga [Anantavarman Chōḍa Gaṅga], Śaka 10[10], regnal year • • • [A.D. 1088?]

inscription.p: Fragmentary. Mentions the temple of Vishṇu. Details lost.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. B. 132 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar set up in front of the Śiva temple on the bank of the Kṛishṇā.

Dated in Śaka 1167 Viśvāvasu, Paushya, ba. 15, Thursday, solar eclipse corresponding to A.D.
1246, January 24. The eclipse seems to have occurred on Friday.

Registers the gift of the royal share rāja-nālu (rāja-pālu) of one puṭṭi [of grain] from the
income from lands irrigated by the tank called Kaṁḍidēvi-cheṟuvu for offerings in the temple at
Ēlēśvaram. Kaṁḍidēvi is mentioned in No. 117 below.

edition.p: Figures of Sun and crescent moon and a liṅga in between.

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. B. 133 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar set up in front of the Śiva temple on the bank of the Kṛishṇā.

Records the service (dāsatvam) of Uttamagaṇḍa Bhīmanātha, son of Kaṁḍidēvi and a servant
of Gaṇḍagōpāla, to god Ēlēśvara for the merit of Gaṇapatidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. B. 134 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar set up in front of the Śiva temple on the bank of the Kṛishṇā.

Seems to register the gift of incomes at the wharf of Ēlīśvaram from the villages, Gummalam,
Krottalūru, Eyipūru etc., for offerings and worship to the god Ēlēśvara by Chāḷukya Tribhuvanamalla
Mallideva.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. B. 135 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar set up in front of the Śiva temple on the bank of the Kṛishṇā.

Records the gift of the village Ambalipalli for offerings and worship of god Ēleśvara by the
king.

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. B. 136 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar set up in front of the Śiva temple on the bank of the Kṛishṇā.

Registers the gift of Cheṭṭa(Pedda?)-Kōḍūru for worship of god Ēlēśvara by Kandūri
Gōkarṇadēvachōḍa-mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. B. 137 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar set up in front of the Śiva temple on the bank of the Kṛishṇā.

Records the gift of the village Kaṁbālapalle to god Ēlēśvara by Mallēṭi (Madduleṭṭi ?) of
Guḍivāḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. B. 138 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar set up in front of the Śiva temple on the bank of the Kṛishṇā.
Dated in Saumya, Phālguna, ba. 11.

Seems to record the gift of the village Taḍimaḍa in Kanna-munnūru by Vēgeyadēva, Mandaya
and Gaṇapaya, who are styled gandhahasti-gajasāhiṇis for the merit of Kākatīśvara Gaṇapatidēva-
mahārāja. The gift-village was entrusted to Bhāvabrahma-gurudēva Sahadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. B. 139 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar in front of the Śiva temple.

Registers the gift of pendōṇṭa (garden) in Rachchamallanipalli to god Ēlēśvara by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Anuṅgu-Kāmarāju Bētarāju. Also records the gift of the village Nārapepalli by
Chakranārāyaṇa Anuṅgurāju.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. B. 140 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar in front of the Śiva temple.

Records the gift of the village Tēkulapalli to god Ēlēśvara by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tribhuvana-
peṇḍāra Manma-Rāmadēva-mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. B. 141 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar in front of the Śiva temple.
Records the gift of the village Velpunūru to go Ēlēśvara by Siddhirāju of Podukamūru.

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. B. 142 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar in front of the Śiva temple.

Records the gift of the village Kūchakōpalli to god Ēlēśvara by Kandūri Bhīmadēvachōḍa-
mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. B. 143 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar in front of the Śiva temple.
Dated in Saumya, Phālguna ba. 11.

Records the gift of the village Garaṁjara or Galaṁjiri in Sindavāḍi-1000 by mahāpradhāni
sarvādhikāri Sāṁva(ba)-paṇḍita for worship and offerings of god Ēlēśvara and for a feeding-house
(satra) for the merit of Siṁhaṇadeva-mahārāja. The gift was made over to Bhāva-brahma on the
specified date. (cf. No. 122- A.R. No. 138 of 1954-55).

Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a granite pillar in front of the Śiva temple.

Seems to record a gift of Nagadonanūru (?) for the merit of the king. The name of the

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. B. 145 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in front of the Śiva temple.

Damaged. Preamble describes the Kākatīya lineage up to Rudradevī (Rudramā) and refers
to the consecration of several liṅgas and to the grant of several villages including Velagapūṁḍi,
Kommūru, Aitaprōlu etc., Viśvēśvaraśivāchārya is mentioned as the manager of the gift-villages.

(Cf. Malkapuram inscription of Rudrāṁbā which was published in S.I.I. X, No. 395 and Journal
of the Andhra Historical Research Society, Vol. IV, pp. 147-164).

edition.p: Rest of the lines are worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. B. 146 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a red-stone slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.

Dated C.V. year 33, Plava, Āshāḍha śu. 11, Wednesday; C.V. 33 equivalent to Śaka 1031 = A.D.
1109, but the cyclic year is Virōdhin. Āshāḍha, śu. 11 fell on Thursday. It corresponds to A.D. 1109,
June 10.

Registers a gift of the village Pedda-Kōḍūru by the king for worship and offerings to god
Ēlēśvara. The king is stated to belong to the solar race and bears the epithet, Kōḍūrupuravarēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. B. 147 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a red-stone slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.

Dated [Śaka] 1179, Piṅgaḷa, Māgha, ba. 15, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1258, January 6. The
week-day was Sunday.
Damaged. Records a gift of 1 puṭṭi of land by Chandaladēvi to god Ēlēśvara.

edition.p: Rest of the lines are badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. B. 148 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a red-stone slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.
Registers a gift of land in Arikatala for a perpetual lamp to god Ēlēśvara by Dori-seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. B. 149 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a red-stone slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.

Registers a gift of 5 ghaṭṭi-Pahiṁḍi (gold coins) to god Ēlēśvara by Muppana for the merit
of his parents Nāvana-bōya and Asana-sāni.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. B. 150 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On one face of the red-stone slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.
Records a gift of 25 cows for a perpetual lamp to god Ēlēśvara by the chief.

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. B. 151 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On one face of the red-stone slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.

Registers a gift of 50 goats for a perpetual lamp in the Ēlēśvara temple by Chenna Kāṭa-boya
for the merit of his parents.

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. B. 153 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the base of a sculpture in front of the Śiva temple.
• • • • [11th century Characters].
Small label containing the words, Mādiseṭṭi Dēvana.

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. B. 157 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a boulder on the way to the ferry from the Śiva temple.
Dated Vṛisha, Bhādrapada śu. 14, Wednesday (Late characters).
Records the obeisance of Gōpāḷuni Narasayya to Gaṅgā-Bhāgīrathī.

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. B. 158 of 1954-55.)
Ēlēśvaram, Devarakonda Taluk, Nalgonda District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a hero-stone in front of a ruined temple on the way to the ferry.
Dated Śaka 978 (= A.D. 1056-57).

Records the death of Mallaya Kāmaṇḍu in a fight on behalf of his master. States that the
memorial stone was the work of Āvegaḍa Sarasvati. Refers to one Vākōju along with Sarasvati.

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1955-56.)
Iruvāram, Chittoor Taluk and District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab fixed into the celling of the Nāgēśvara temple.

The record is dated Kali 4953, Śālivāhana śaka 1775, Paridhāvin, corresponding to 1853 A.D.
Pramādīcha could be the cyclic year, and it lasts upto 9th March, 1853. In the A.R. for 1955-56, the
Kali year was wrongly noted as 4653.
[Seems to state that the mukha-maṇḍapa was built or renovated on the specified date.]

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1955-56.)
Mēḍikurti, Vayalpad Taluk, Chittoor District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Lakshmīnarasiṁha temple.

Dated Śaka 1530, Kīlaka, Kārtika, śu. 15, corresponding to 1608 A.D., November 11, Friday. (In
the A.R. for 1955-56, the Śaka year was wrongly noted as 1533).

Registers that the vidvan-mahājanas, the village officials and Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju
Kōnēṭi Timmarājayyadēva mahārāja agreed among themselves that the levy of grain at a specified
rate collected on all the lands in the village, be utilized for maintaining the tanks, ponds and the
canals of the village in the proportion of the irrigated areas under each of them.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1955-56.)
Mēḍikurti, Vayalpad Taluk, Chittoor District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab at the entrance of the Lakshmīnarasiṁha temple.
. • • • • (In characters of 13th century).

Damaged. Mentions Śāntaśiva-dēśika of Nava-Gōḷakī-maṭha and seems to refer to a gift of
land made for his memorial.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1955-56.)
Mēḍikurti, Vayalpad Taluk, Chittoor District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone in Survey No. 959.
Dated Śaka 1302, Raudri, Māgha śu. 1, Friday, corresponding to A.D. 1380, December 28.

Damaged. Registers a gift of land to Basavaṁṇa, son of Dēvaṁṇa of Gautama-gotra and
Ṛik-śākhā by the Chief.

edition.p: Figures of Sun and crescent-moon at the beginning and ending respectively of line 1.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1955-56.)
Mēḍikurti, Vayalpad Taluk, Chittoor District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab lying near the Śiva temple.
Dated Śaka 1303, Durmati, Pushya śu. 1, Tuesday, corresponding to A.D. 1381, December 17.

Registers a gift of land in Mēḍikuru situated in Penugoṇḍa-Mārjavāḍi by the chief to
Ōbaḷanātha.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1955-56.)
Chēgireḍḍipāḍu, Bezwada Taluk, Krishna District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: Slab on the bund of the irrigation canal to the west of the village.

States that the well was the gift of Sundarayya, Subbayya and Achchayya, sons of Veṁkanna
of Garigepāḍu. (Written in late characters).

edition.p: Figures of Conch, tirunāmam and discus.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1955-56.)
Saṅgam, Kovur Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the tiers of the entrance to the Saṅgamēśvara temple.

Dated Śaka 11[2]6, Rudhirōdgārin, Āśvija. The cyclic year corresponds to Śaka. 1125 and Āśvija
corresponds to 8th September-6th October of 1203 A.D.

Registers a gift of a perpetual lamp by Nallagāmaka, the queen of Nallasiddhirāju, to the god
Saṅgāmudēva.

l.te: gā beṭṭenu | ·i (·ī) dha¨rmmuvu māhēśvara rakṣamaṁgaḷamahā śrī [|]
śrīmatu nallakētamaraḍḍi koḍuku dāmana peṭṭina saṁdadīpa (saṁdhyādīpamu) |

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1955-56.)
Saṅgam, Kovur Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the tiers of the entrance to the Saṅgamēśvara temple.
Dated Śaka 1140, Phālguṇa, ba. 3, Tuesday, corresponding to A.D. 1219, March 5.

Fragmentary. Seems to refer to some grant made by Polī-raḍi-Surapa-raḍi-Bhīmana for the
merit of his mother (Cf. NDI, Part II, Nellore, No. 103).

edition.p: (The inscription ends here).

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1955-56.)
Saṅgam, Kovur Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the tiers of the entrance to the Saṅgamēśvara temple.

Dated Śaka [1149], Chaitra śu. 7, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1227, March 25. The cyclic
year is Sarvajit. [The Govt. Epigraphist could not trace the Śaka year on the stone, but NDI, Part II,
No. 102 refers to the Śaka year. By the time of the visit of the Epigraphical officer, the stone could
have peeled off.]
Damaged. Seems to record some gift by Bhaima • • la-sāni for the merit of her husband.

edition.p: Rest of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1955-56.)
Saṅgam, Kovur Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the right wall of the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.

Dated Śaka 1473, Virōdhikṛit, Chaitra ba. 7, Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1551, March 28. (The
week day appears to be Saturday).

Registers a gift of the income derived from palmgrove raised at Saṅgam by Rāmireḍḍi,
son of Koṇḍā-Mummaḍireḍḍi of Duvvūru, for the merit of Baicharājayya administering over the
Udayagiri-rājya, the nāyaṅkara of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju Yaram-Tirumalayyadēva-mahārāju. Also
registers another gift of land at Navūru and other villages in the nāyaṅkara of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Apratīkamalla Parichchhedi Mummaya Dēvachōḍa-mahārāja. (Cf. N.D.I., Part II, No. 104).

edition.p: (The inscription ends here.)

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1955-56.)
Saṅgam, Kovur Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the stones built into the prākāra wall of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.
Dated Śaka 1539, Piṅgaḷa, Jyēshṭha śu. 15, corresponding to A.D. 1617, June 9.

States that Nalla-Guruvayya, son of Peḍḍi-batta and Virūpākshamma and grandson of Pōtā-batta
of Poṅgūr belonging to Viśvakarma-gōtra, consecrated Kāśī-Viśvanātha along with some subsidiary
deities. Contains below the record, the names of devotees: Aṅgappa Ekāmbaram, Yeraguruvu and
Bālaguruvappa.

l.te: vighnēśvara [|] svasti śrī śālivāhana śakaṁ 1539 ·agu piṁgaḷa saṁvvatsara jyēṣṭha-
śudha(ddha) 15 lu rājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrī komāra veṁkaṭrāyalu rā-
jyaṁ śāyaga viśva[ka]¨rma gōtra poṁgguri pōtābattu pautrapeddibatta puttra virūpākṣa-
[ṁma] tanūbhava śrī nallaguruvayya śrī kāśī viśvanāthādi parivāra sahita pratiṣṭha [||]

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1955-56.)
Saṅgam, Kovur Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On an inside wall of the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.
Dated Śaka 1775, Parīdhāvi (A.D. 1853).
States that the mukha-maṇḍapa and other accessories were built on the specified year.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1955-56.)
Saṅgam, Kovur Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the tiers of the basement of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.
Records the perpetual devotion of Nalla-Guruvayya [Cf. No. 149 AR. No. 15 of 1955-56].

edition.p:
[Nos. 152-240 from Sālihuṇḍām contain the pot-sherd label inscriptions discovered in the course
of excavations at Sālihuṇḍām, Srikakulam taluk, Srikakulam district (in Photographs). The language of
the inscriptions is Prakrit and the script is Brāhmī of the early Centuries of the Chrishian era. As
the content is small, the texts of the inscriptions are given with reference to Photograph numbers,
and the probable dates on palaeographical grounds].

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1955-56.)
Maḍutuṟṟu, Sarvasiddhi Taluk, Visakhapatnam District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab fixed into the wall of the Bhīmēśvara temple.
inscription.h4: Anantavarman Rājarāja (III) of the Eastern Gangas.
inscription.p:
Dated in his 1st regnal year and Śaka 1119, Kanyā-māsa śu. 11, Tuesday, corresponding to A.D.
1197, September 23.

Records the installation of the deity Chōḍīśvara-mahādēva and the grants of lands for
maintaining services in the temple by Prōli-seṭṭi and Nāmi-seṭṭi belonging to a merchant-guild (?)
called seṭṭikāra.
(Published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XLI, pp. 214 ff.).

edition.p:
[* Lines 45-46 are engraved at the top of the First face of the inscription above the
Nandi figure].

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1956-57.)
Ryāli, Kottapeta Taluk, East Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the right jamb of the main shrine of the Umākamaṇḍalēśvarasvāmin temple.
This is dated Śaka 10[71], Uttarāyaṇa Sankrānti (A.D. 1149).
Damaged. Records the gift of a lamp and refers to Paṇḍayārāja and Komma-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1956-57.)
Ryāli, Kottapeta Taluk, East Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Umākamaṇḍalēśvarasvāmin temple.
This is dated Śaka 1223, Vishuva-Sankrānti (A.D. 1301).
Badly damaged. Refers to some construction in the temple of Mūlasthānadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1956-57.)
Ryāli, Kottapeta Taluk, East Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Umākamaṇḍalēśvarasvāmin temple.
Undated. (In late characters).
Records the obeisance of Penumuchchu (Penumatsa) Timmarāja to god Paramēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1956-57.)
Ryāli, Kottapeta Taluk, East Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Umākamaṇḍalēśvarasvāmin temple.
This is dated Ānanda, Kārttika, śu. 10. Written in late characters.
Records some gift to the shrine of the goddess.

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1956-57.)
Narsāpur, Narsapur Taluk, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up outside the Madanagōpālasvāmin temple.
inscription.h4: Vīrabhadra of the Chāḷukyas of Niḍadavōlu.
inscription.p: This is dated in the year 15, uttarāyaṇa Sankrānti.

Registers the gift of a perpetual lamp to the temple of Mārkaṇḍēśvara-mahādēva at
Rājamahēndrapura for which 50 inupa-yeḍḷu (buffaloes) were given by Pōtāsāni, daughter of
Potamāṟēni(?). The buffaloes were entrusted to Kētana-bōya, son of Pōtana-pāṅgu for the daily supply
of ghee measuring one sāniya-mānika to the temple.

l.te: svasti sa¨rvvalōkā[śraya] śrī viṣṇuva¨rddhana mahārājulaina vīrabhadrēśvaradēva cakrava-
dīpamunaku na • • • pōtanapāṁgu koḍku kētanabōyuni vasamuna

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1956-57.)
Narsāpur, Narsapur Taluk, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up outside the Madanagōpālasvāmin temple.

This is dated in the regnal year 24, chaitra, śu. 13, Thursday, Vishu-sankrānti. (A.D. 1159, April
2; Sankrānti occurred on 24th March).

Records a gift of 50 buffaloes for the supply of one māna (mānika) of ghee daily for lighting
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mārkaṇḍēśvara-mahādēva of Rājamahēndravara located in Śakaṭa-
manthani-nāṇḍu, by Sūri-seṭṭi, son of Nāgi-seṭṭi and grandson of Allāḍa-seṭṭi of Jananāthapura. The
buffaloes were entrusted to Rēya-bōya, son of Eṟiya-bōya. In addition to this, the donor seems to have
gifted one puṭṭi of land.

l.te: rājya saṁvatsara[ṁbulu] 24 gu nēṇṭi caitra śu-

edition.p: Blank space.

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1956-57.)
Narsāpur, Narsapur Taluk, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up outside the Madanagōpālasvāmin temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1220, Pushya ba. 7, Thursday, uttarāyaṇa-sankrānti. May correspond to
A.D., 1298, December 25.

Records a gift of 50 buffaloes for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mārkaṇḍēśvara at
Rājamahēndrapura by Pōta-sāni, daughter of Pōtakaṟēni (Cf. No. 246- A.R. No. 8 of 1956-57) and wife
of Malli-nēḍu for her own merit. The buffaloes were entrusted to Prōle-bōya, son of Malle-bōya.

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1956-57.)
Narsāpur, Narsapur Taluk, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up outside the Madanagōpālasvāmin temple.

This is dated in the king's regnal year 35 in Śaka 1097, Chaitra, Vishuva-sankrānti, corresponding
to A.D. 1175, March 25.

Records a gift of 25 buffaloes for half a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mārkaṇḍīśvara-
mahādēva by Komari-raḍḍi for the merit of his father Dōrama-raḍḍi. The buffaloes were entrusted to
Eṟiyama-bōya, son of Aṁga Kāpana, stipulating the quantity of ghee to be supplied for the lamp at
an aḍḍa every day by the sāniya-māna. The inscription was written by Naṁganasari (Nanganāchāri
?), who probably incised the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1956-57.)
Narsāpur, Narsapur Taluk, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured panel on a pillar in the Madanagōpālasvāmin temple.
Undated, but in characters of 12th century A.D.

States that the liṅga was installed by Pāśupata-ayyaṁgāru. The panel depicts a figure, evidently,
the person mentioned in the record, seated by the side of the nandi in front of which is depicted
the liṅga installed by him.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1956-57.)
Narsāpur, Narsapur Taluk, West Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured panel on a pillar in the Madanagōpālasvāmin temple.
Undated, but in characters of 12th-13th century A.D.

States that the liṅga was installed by Śiṅgana. The panel depicts a figure, evidently the person
mentioned in the record, seated by the side of the nandi in front of which is depicted the liṅga
installed by him.

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1956-57.)
Guntur, Guntur Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: Photograph and impression from Sri P Seshadri Sastri, Guntur.
Findspot: Unknown.
Prākṛit inscription in Brāhmī script assignable to 2nd century A.D.

Mentions Thēra Budhi who had attained Parinivṛitti. Also refers to Chaṁdā (Chandrā),
Siddhathi(Siddhārthī) and another as the daughters of a person (name lost).

l.prtr:
Siddham namo Bhagavato [|*] Parinivatasa therasa Budhisa Vaniyiniya Chakhanipave •
• • • • •
dhatunaṁ Chaṁdāya Sidhathiya Chēbu-dhāya cha • • • kasa Budhi

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1956-57.)
Uṇḍavalli, Guntur Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the verandah of the Bhīmēśvara temple.
This is dated in the 10th regnal year, and Śaka 1055, solar eclipse. (A.D. 1133).

Records the installation of Bhīmēśvara at Uṇḍavelli by Kaṇḍravāḍi Bhīmarāja and
registers a gift of two Khaṇḍugas of land by him for worship and offerings in the temple. Also
records the construction of a shrine for Gaṇapati in the Śiva temple by Paṇḍi-raḍḍi, son of Kūchena-
raḍḍi of Uṇḍavelli.

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1956-57.)
Uṇḍavalli, Guntur Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the mutilated base of an octagonal pillar found in the village.
Dated in Śaka 1[0*?]77, uttarāyaṇa Sankrānti. (= A.D. 1155 (?)).

Fragmentary and damaged. Seems to refer to Āchārya Śrīvallabha of Uṇḍavelli, who is
described as Tribhuvanaguru and as a recipient of some gift (details lost).

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1956-57.)
Vaikuṇṭhapuram, Guntur Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a rock face on the west of the Veṅkaṭēśvara temple on a hill.

Dated in Śaka 1197, Bhāva, Jyēshṭha, ba. 5, Saturday (?), corresponding to A.D. 1274 May 26.
(Bhāva corresponds to Śaka 1196).

Damaged. Seems to record a gift of 1 Khaṇḍuga of land to god Bhairava of Vaikuṇṭhapuram.
Mentions Ānandanātha, probably as the donor of the grant.

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1956-57.)
Vaikuṇṭhapuram, Guntur Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On one face of the rock on the west of the Veṅkaṭēśvara temple.
Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 13th century A.D.
Damaged. Refers to a person (name lost) as a servant of Perumāḷḷu.

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1956-57.)
Peṭlūrupālem, Narasaraopet Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in a field about 2 miles from the main road on the way to Kōṭappakoṇḍa.

Dated in Śaka 1075, Śrīmukha, uttarāyaṇa-sankrānti. May correspond to A.D. 1153, December
24.

Damaged. Seems to record the construction of a tank and an endowment for the havis, bali
and archana of god Trikūṭīśvaradēva by a servant of Buddana, son of Dāmōdara and Dāramāṁbā,
of Harita-gōtra.

l.te: na [|] svadattāṁ para-dattāṁ vā yō ha-

edition.p:
(—) in the middle of the lines indicates gap in writing. Could it be that it was probably
the punctuation mark for virāma and was placed wrongly ?

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1956-57.)
Ongole, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the compound of the Kāśīviśvanātha temple.
Dated in Śaka 1212, uttarāyaṇa-sankrānti (= A.D. 1290).

Damaged. Registers a gift of land to god Chōḍa-Gōpīnātha of Oṅgōlu by the chief for the
merit of his parents. Sāraṅga is mentioned as the sthānika and Brammi-reḍḍi and others as signatories.
(Published in Butterworth & Venugopal chetty. NDI, Part III, p. 1082 without separating the lines).

edition.p: [Figures of Sun and Moon, and below are conch, deity (?) and discus.]

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1956-57.)
Uppuguṇḍūru, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in front of the Śiva temple.
Dated Śaka 1140, Kārttīka, punnami, Kṛittika, Sunday but datable to A.D. 1218, November 4.

Partly built in. Records the construction of the big tank in the village by Aitama-seṭṭi, the
Prabhu-mukhya of Nellūru-sthala situated in Pāka-nāḍu 21,000 and the son of Miṇḍa-guddali Chaṁḍi-
seṭṭi. (cf. NDI, Part III, pp. 1129-31).

edition.p: Figures of moon, liṅga, Sun and Nāga.

Lines 8-10 are available in NDI, III, pp. 1130-31, while this portion is built in, according to the
Govt. Epigraphist.

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1956-57.)
Uppuguṇḍūru, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone in the Śiva temple.

Dated in [Śaka] 11[7]0, Kīlaka, Adhika Jyēshṭha (may correspond to 27th April-24th May, A.D.
1248).
Fragmentary. Seems to mention Gaurīśvaradēva. Other details are lost.

edition.p: Figures of Sun and crescent Moon.
(rest is built in).

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1956-57.)
Bellamkoṇḍavāripālem (hamlet of Nāgulavaram), Palnad Taluk, Guntur District,
Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab on the roadside about 6 miles from Nāgārjunakoṇḍa.
Dated in Śaka 1438, Dhāta, Āshāḍha, śu. 11, Tuesday, corresponding to A.D. 1516, June 10.

Registers a grant of the villages Guṇḍāla and Malyaguṇḍla situated in the Nāgārjunikoṇḍa-
sīma to god Guṇḍāla-Tirumaladēva by Śaṅkhanāyini-Basavānāyinigāru, for the merit of his paternal
uncle Tipi-nēḍu, aunt Chennama, father Nāgi-nēḍu, mother Annama, and his young brothers Bhīmayya
and Mādayya. States that Nāgārjunikoṇḍa-sīma was held by the donor as nāyaṅkara obtained from
Sāḷuva Timmarasayya.

edition.p: Figures of moon, Garutman(?), discus, tīrunāmam, serpent, conch, sage and sun.

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1956-57.)
Koppunūru, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar on the roadside about 7 miles from Mācherla.
Dated in Śaka 1056, Ānanda, Pushya, solar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1135, January 16.

Gives a string of titles of a chief (name lost) and seems to refer to the installation of a staṁ
bha by him. (Cf. A.R. No. 216 of 1927).

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar from site No. 13 (Neg. No. 273/55), preserved at the local museum.

Written in Prakrit language and Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D. (bears the date: year 11
varshā, paksha 1, divasa 8).
Memorial pillar for Vaṁmabhaṭa, mother of Rudrapurushadatta.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXIV, pp. 20 ff).

l.prtr: vāsā pakhaṁ pathamaṁ 1 divasaṁ aṭhamaṁ 8 saga-gatāya chhāya

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured pillar in site No. NK (V)/55 with two figures riding on an elephant.
Undated, but palaeographically datable to 3rd century A.D.
Memorial pillar for Chaṁtamūla of the Kuḷahaka family.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXV, p. 14).

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a sculptured lintel lying in trenches C 4 and D 4 in Division 180 of sector No. 2(?).

This inscription is written in the Sanskrit language and the Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D.
It is dated in the king's regnal year 24, varshā, paksha 4, divasa 5.
Records that the stone image of Buddha was installed by a Śrēshṭhin Kumāranandin.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXV, pp. 11-13).

l.skttr:
Sidhaṁ siddham [||] mahārājasya Ikshvākunāṁ(ṇāṁ) śrī-Ehavala-Chantamūlasya
samva(saṁva)tsaraṁ chaturvvīśaṁ varsha-pakshaṁ chatu[rttha]ṁ divasaṁ pañchamaṁ
Śrī[parvvate] [Vijaya]pū(pu)ryyāṁ Ma[hā]devī-[pa]rive[ṇ]e Iru[sakā(?)]nāṁ[kula]prasū
[tasya] Ba[raṇa?]kīyānāṁ(nām) I(I)[śvarada]ttasya putreṇa Syandaka-parvvata-śreshṭhinā
Ku[mā]ranandinā saha bharyyāya śreshṭhinīya putreṇa cha Iśvarabharttinā śesheṇa cha
svajana-sambandhi-varggeṇa iyaṁ bha[ga]vatoḥ(taḥ) sartva(rvva)-satv[o](ttv-o)tta[masya]
sarvva-guṇa-pārami(mī)-prāptasya samyak-sambuddhasya Buddha[s]ya śailamayī pratimā
pratishṭhāpitā ātmaṇaḥ sarvva-satvā(ttvā)nāñ=cha hita-[su]kh-ārtthaṁ(rttham ||) bhavati
ch=ātra ślokā(kaḥ) | [Deya]-dharmmam=ida(ma)ṅ=kṛitvā yat=puṇyaṁ samupārjjitaṁ(tam)
| te[na] puṇyeṇa(na) loko=yaṁ nir[vv]āṇam=adhigachchhatu ||

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a broken pillar in Division 109 of sector No. 3.

Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 3rd century A.D. Written in Prākṛit language and
Brāhmī script.
Fragmentary and damaged. Refers to a pillar (as a gift ?).

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a broken pillar in Division 109 of sector No. 3.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D.
Refers to the gift of a pillar.

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a broken pillar in Division 109 of sector No. 3.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D.
Erection of a memorial pillar for Prince Karadaru, who was also a sēnāpati.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXV, pp. 15-16).

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a broken pillar in Division 109 of sector No. 3.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D.
Memorial pillar for a military general • • ṭapuṭa
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXV, p. 15).

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a broken pillar in Division 109 of sector No. 3.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D.
Records the erection of a memorial pillar for a raṭhika, resident of Magalaraṇa.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXV, p. 16).

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a broken pillar in Division 109 of sector No. 3.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D.
Memorial pillar for the dead soldiers who were under the command of a rāshṭrika.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXV, p. 15).

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. 35 of 1956-57.)
Nāgārjunakoṇḍa, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone Buddha-pāda discovered from site No. NK V (VI)/1955, kept in the Antiquities section.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 3rd century A.D.

Refers to the installation of the pair of the feet of lord (Buddha) in the monastery of theiyas and
Vibhajjavādins.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXIII, pp. 247 ff).

l.pr:
Sidhaṁ [|*] āchariyanaṁ Theriyānaṁ Vibhaja-vādānaṁ Kasmira-Gaṁdhāra-Yavana-
Vanavāsa-Taṁbapaṁnidipa-pasādakanaṁ
vihāre Bhagavato pāda-saṁghāḍā nipatiṭhapito sava-satānaṁ hita-sukh-athanāya ti

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1956-57.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On an octagonal pillar preserved in the Archaeological Museum.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 2nd century A.D.
Refers to an artisan.

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1956-57.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar preserved in the Archaeological Museum.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 2nd century B.C.
Refers to (the gift of a) pillar by Khalāta.

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1956-57.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar preserved in the Archaeological Museum.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 2nd century B.C.
Refers to the gift of a pillar by Utara, son of Achina, and by Khalāta.

l.pr: Achinaka putēna Utarasa Khalātasa cha thabhō

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1956-57.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of a sculpture kept in the Archaeological Museum.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 1st century A.D.
Damaged. Refers to a gift by a merchant (Vāniya) Hakana (?).

l.pr: • • • • • la vāniyasa ma. sa ma. ya

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1956-57.)
Amarāvati, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a fragment of a slab kept in the Archaeological Museum.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 2nd century A.D.
Purport not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1956-57.)
Ghaṇṭasāla, Divi Taluk, Krishna District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab in the village.
Written in Prākṛit language and Brāhmī script of 1st century A.D.
Mentions gift by lay-worshipper who was the mother of Sivala (?).

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1956-57.)
Giddalūru, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On pillar No. 1 of the Pātāḷa-Nāgēśvara temple.

This is dated Pārthiva, Pushya, śu. 3, Sunday, probably corresponding to A.D. 1525, December
17.

Registers a gift of wet land and 2 tūmu of dry land by Timmi-śeṭṭi, son of Vuppara Pegaḍa-
śeṭṭi to Tipana, son of Chinta Chenne-bōya for his having constructed the wall round the temple of
Kēśava-perumāḷ.

l.te: ṁ pā¨rtthiva saṁvvatsara puṣya śu 3 ·ā [|] śrīmaṁ-

edition.p: The rest broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1956-57.)
Giddalūru, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On pillar No. 8 of the Pātāḷa-Nāgēśvara temple.

Dated Pramādin, Āshāḍha śu. • • • • (in late characters). (Cyclic year wrongly read as Virōdhin
in A.R. 1956-57).

Enjoins the temple-servants, not to touch the god without taking a bath, and the worshippers,
not to leave their foot-wear in the temple in front of god Kēśavarāya lest they go to hell.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1956-57.)
Giddalūru, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On the Nandi pillar of the Pāṭaḷa-Nāgēśvara temple.

Dated Śaka 1343, Vijaya, Mārgaśira, śu. 11, Sunday, (Irregular). If the cyclic year is true, it
corresponds to Ś. 1335 and the details tally for A.D. 1413, December 3.
Damaged. Refers to some gift for the merit of the king (name lost).

edition.p: (Rest completely worn out).

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1956-57.)
Giddalūru, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Pāṭaḷa-Nāgēśvara temple.

Dated Śaka • • • ., Plava, Āśvija, śu. 10, in characters of the 15th century A.D. (Space for Śaka
year left blank).

Registers a gift of land by the Reḍḍis and Karaṇams of Gṛiddalūru to Kāśe Honnūrappa of
Guḍipāḍu for his having built the temple and installed the image therein.

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1956-57.)
Giddalūru, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Pāṭaḷa-Nāgēśvara temple.
This is an undated inscription in late characters.

Records the obeisance of Mūla Rāghava-bhaṭlu, son of Yaṟu (Yeṟṟa)-bhaṭlu and grandson of
Jagu-bhaṭlu to god Pātāḷa-Nāgēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1956-57.)
Gōpanipalle (hamlet of Komaravōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar built into the wall of the temple of Gōpīnātha.

Dated Śaka 1449, Sarvajit, Mārgaśira śu. 15, Saturday, corresponding to A.D. 1527, December
7.

Damaged. Records a gift of land to god Gōpīnātha-Perumāḷ by Pōti-nēḍu, son of Timmā-
nāyuḍu and grandson of Chōḍa Pōti-nāyuḍu for the daśamī festival.

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1956-57.)
Satvēl, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On pillar I in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.
The inscription is undated, but palaeographically assignable to 8th century A.D.
Label inscription mentioning an ascetic.

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1956-57.)
Satvēl, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.
Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 8th century A.D.

Seems to refer to the stone as Santanāntara Vyaḷi-aḷampu-śila. The ruler could be of the
Chālukya family.

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1956-57.)
Satvēl, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.
Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 8th century A.D.
Mentions the name of an individual.

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1956-57.)
Satvēl, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.
Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 7th century A.D.
Refers to some title, utpatti piḍugu.

edition.p: (Below are the figures of moon, aṅkuśa (?) and sun).

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1956-57.)
Satvēl, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.
Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 8th century A.D.
Purport not clear.

edition.p: (Rest effaced).

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1956-57.)
Satvēl, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.
Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 8th century A.D.
Titles of an individual.

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1956-57.)
Satvēl, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.
Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 10th century A.D.
Refers to one Ko(u)māra-Kattara.

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1956-57.)
Karimaddula, Nandyal Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab paved into the floor around the darga (mosque).

Dated Śaka 1498 (wrong for 1488), Kshaya, Āshāḍha, śu. 12 corresponding to A.D. 1566, June
29.

Registers a gift of land as sarvamānya to goddess Akkaladēvī of Karimaddula by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Timmayadēva-mahārāja of the Ātrēya-gōtra, on the occasion of utthāna-dvādaśī.
The gift-land lay in Karimaddula in Poṭlapāḍu in Peḍakaṇṭi-sīma which was given to the donor as
nāyaṅkara by the king.

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1956-57.)
Karimaddula, Nandyal Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab paved into the floor around the darga (mosque).
Damaged and undated.

Seems to record a gift of land for a garden to the goddess Aṁkāladēvamma, probably by
the donor Timmayadeva-mahārāja mentioned in the inscription (No. 292- A.R. No. 60 of 1956-57).

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1956-57.)
Darsi, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab lying near the image of Āñjaneya.

Dated Śaka 1235, Pramādīcha, Kārtīka, śu. 15, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1313, November.
3.

Seems to record a gift of land for a garden to the god Tripurantaka Mahadeva by Mallaṇa-
devaṇṇa with the permission of Potaya-sāhiṇi.

edition.p: Figures of Cresent moon and sun.

l.te: pramādīca saṁvatsara kā¨rtīka śu 15 gu svasti śrīmanma[hāma]-
ṁḍalēśvara kākatiyya pratāpa rudradēva mahārājula ja • • • • •
ri dakṣiṇānanu mōṭirēgaṭanu kha 3 na 16 podukamūri

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1956-57.)
Darsi, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone set up in front of the Śiva temple.
Dated Rudhirōdgāri, Vishu-sankrānti.

Registers gifts of land altogether ten puṭṭis in areas in three villages as sarvamānya by
Nāgadēvarāja as bhōgavṛitti to god Svayaṁbhūdēva of Darśi for the merit of the king as well as of
Jaṟudēva, the donor's father. (See. N.D.I. Part I, p. 29, Darśi 10).

edition.p:
Figures of liṅga at the top, sun and moon in the middle, and bull at the bottom and
vatsa in the rear.

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1956-57.)
Darsi, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up on the bund of Achchanna tank.
Dated Śaka 13[5]7, Rākshasa, Māgha śu. 15, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1436, February 2.

Records the construction of a tank by the chief in the name of his mother, Āryamādēvi and
the grant of an agrahāra and other benefactions for the merit of his ancestors, his son and others.
(Published in N.D.I. Part I, p. 297, Darśi 13).

[The first two sides of the inscription are considered by the Epigraphist's office as constituting
one inscription while the third side as another (No. 297- A.R. No. 65 of 1956-57). All the three sides
are put together as one inscription in the N.D.I. pp. 27 ff. N.D.I. reads king's name as Aśnadēva but
Annadēva may be correct. The inscriptions (A.R. 64 and 65- Nos. 296-297) are badly damaged and
reading and purport unintelligible, while the NDI has better readings, though not verifiable. Readers
are advised to consult N.D.I. Part I, pp. 297 ff.]

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1956-57.)
Darsi, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up on the bund of Achchanna tank.

[Undated, but likely of the inscription of the same chief noticed in No. 296- A.R. No. 64 of
1956-57. This inscription was noted in NDI as a continuation of the inscription noticed as A.R. No.
64 of 1956-57].

Records the construction of the temple of Gōpīnātha in the name of Annadēvarāja, and a
tank in the name of his mother, Lakshmīdēvi by Potarāju Mantrirāja (See N.D.I. Part I, p. 302).

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1956-57.)
Donakoṇḍa, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the temple of Gangamma.

Dated Śaka 1315 (1328), Vyaya, Kārttika, ba. 5, Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1406, October 31.
[Authors of N.D.I. read 1328, while the figures look like 1315. However as Vyaya corresponds to Śaka
1328, and as the astonomical details agree for Śaka 1328, that date may be accepted].

Records that somebody undertook the construction of the temple for Māṁkanama (Mahaṁ
kāḷamma ?), shrine for heroes (Vīrula-guḍi) and compound-wall, expending 195 ḍha(ṭa)ṁkas of money
when Kaṁpadēvani Chāma-voḍeya was ruling over the village. (See N.D.I. D. 19, pp. 301 ff).

[This record, as well as No. 299- A.R. No. 67 of 1956-57 are on a white marble pillar with
a half lotus design on one of its side faces, and fluted into an octogan above the design, similar to
the āyaka pillars of Nāgārjunakoṇḍa type].

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1956-57.)
Donakoṇḍa, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the temple of Gangamma.
Dated Nala, Nija Āśvīja, śu. 15 (Written in late characters).

States that Pōtaya, son of Veṅgaḷa, the priest performed the jaṁḍalu services (?) in the temple.
(See N.D.I. Part I, Darśi 17, p. 308).

edition.p: [See note for No. 298- A.R. No. 66 of 1956-57].

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1956-57.)
Donakoṇḍa, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the temple of Gangamma.

Dated Śaka 13[49] Plavaṅga, Māgha śu. 15, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1428, February 1.
The weekday however was Sunday.

Records the renewal of some cash grant (details lost) made to the goddess Nūkalā-Paramēśvarī
of Donakoṇḍa on a former date (Kārttika, śu. 1), by Rāmayadēva-mahārāja, son of aṁtyambaragaṇḍa
Gumuṇḍūri Kāmayadēva-mahārāja. Donakoṇḍa is stated to be situated in Pina-Ēṟuva, the nāyaṅkara
territory of the donor. (See N.D.I., Part I, Darśi 23, p. 319).

[The N.D.I., gives the text running up to line 35, while by the time when the impressions
were taken by the Epigraphical branch, the part relating to lines 20-35 had faded].

edition.p: Figures of Crescent moon and sun.
Badly worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1956-57.)
Donakoṇḍa, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the temple of Gangamma.

Dated Śaka 135[4], Parīdhāvin, Kārttika, śu. 11, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1432, November
3. The weekday however was Monday.

Damaged. Seems to record the gift of certain incomes from the festivals (details lost), made
to the goddess by • • • rāmayya (NDI, Part I, p. 313, D 20).

l.note:
TheNDI, on pp. 313-14, reads some lines and refers the inscription to the reign of
Dēvarāya in line 12. It could be an inscription of Dēvarāya II.

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1956-57.)
Donakoṇḍa, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the temple of Gangamma.

Dated Śaka 1447, Pārthiva, Pushya śu. 15, Friday, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1525,
December 29.

Registers the grant of Donakoṇḍa by the king to the goddess Gaṅgāparamēśvarī of the village
on the representation of the dvārapālakas Timmapa-nāyuḍu, Āḍapa-nāyuḍu, Bhaṇḍāramu Bokkisam
Bairi-nēḍu, Avasaram Basivi-nēḍu etc. (NDI Part I, p. 315).

l.te: pati gajapati vibhāḷa śrī vīrapratāpa śrī [kṛ]-
ru || maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1956-57.)
Donakoṇḍa, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p:
On a pavement of a slab in the Gaṅgamma temple.
. • • • .
Undated, but palaeographically assignable to 8th century A.D.
Seems to refer to an installation (details lost).

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1956-57.)
Donakoṇḍa, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p:
On a slab built into the shrine on the north side of the Gaṅgamma temple.
. • • • .
Dated Nala, Chaitra, śu. • • • (written in late characters).
Damaged and worn out. Mentions Pagaḍāla Ko • • .

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1956-57.)
Mōpūru, Venkatagiri Division, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the Nāgaliṅgēśvarasvāmin temple.
. • • • .
Dated Raktākshi, Mārgaśira, ba. [10]. Written in late characters.

Fragmentary. Seems to record the gift of land made to one Padmanābhayya. Donor's name
and other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1956-57.)
Chiṭṭivalasa, Visakhapatnam Taluk, Visakhapatnam District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone kept in the Travellers' Bungalow.

Dated year 5, Mēsha-saṅkrānti, Tuesday. (Written in Telugu-Kannaḍa characters of 11th-12th
century A.D.).

Records the allotment of a specified share of the income from a certain levy towards
maintenance of perpetual lamps in the temple of god Gokarṇēśvara-mahādēva of Guḍivāḍa in Barika
alias Uttamagaṅga-dēsiyyakoṇḍa-paṭṭaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1956-57.)
Chiṭṭivalasa, Visakhapatnam Taluk, Visakhapatnam District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone kept in the Travellers' Bungalow.

Dated year 11, uttarāyaṇa Sankrānti (Written in Telugu-Kannaḍa characters of 11th-12th century
A.D.).

Records the gift of two puṭṭis of cultivable land for food offerings to god Gōkarṇa-
Rājanarāyaṇēśvara-mahādēva of Guḍivāḍa, by Kommana, with the consent of Jaddepa-nāyaka, (son of)
Chāmi-nāyaka, the holder of a vṛitti in the temple. (Cf. 308 below).

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1956-57.)
Chiṭṭivalasa, Visakhapatnam Taluk, Visakhapatnam District, Andhra Pradesh.
inscription.p: On a stone kept in the Travellers Bungalow.

Dated year 11, uttarāyaṇa-Sankrānti (Written in Telugu-Kannaḍa characters of 11th-12th century
A.D.)

Records the gift of the village under his enjoyment (bhukti) to god Gōkarṇēśvaradēva of
Guḍivāḍa by Jeddama-nāyaka for food-offerings of the deity.

edition.h4: Second Face (Figures of linga and two lamp-stands on either side).
